title: Zone Boundary Traffic Monitoring — Anomalous Inter-Zone Volume

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 5.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.46: Zone Boundary Traffic Monitoring — Anomalous Inter-Zone Volume.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.46' running on index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" with src_zone, dest_zone, bytes, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-14d
| eval zone_pair=concat(src_zone,"->",dest_zone)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes) as b, dc(dest_port) as ports by _time, zone_pair, app
| eventstats median(b) as med by zone_pair, app
| eval ratio=if(med>0, round(b/med,2), null())
| where ratio>8 AND b>10485760
| sort - b
description: Baselines permitted bytes and sessions between OT zones and flags spikes that may indicate policy drift, covert channels, or misrouted traffic at conduits.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes fw and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Build `ot_zone_pairs_expected.csv` for pairs that should exist; (2) exclude known maintenance windows; (3) alert on new `app` on sensitive pairs; (4) correlate with change records. Indexes required: `index=fw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`.
visualization: Time chart (bytes by zone_pair), Heatmap (zone_pair × hour), Table (top spikes).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=fw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`, `bytes`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Build `ot_zone_pairs_expected.csv` for pairs that should exist; (2) exclude known maintenance windows; (3) alert on new `app` on sensitive pairs; (4) correlate with change records.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-14d
| eval zone_pair=concat(src_zone,"->",dest_zone)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes) as b, dc(dest_port) as ports by _time, zone_pair, app
| eventstats median(b) as med by zone_pair, app
| eval ratio=if(med>0, round(b/med,2), null())
| where ratio>8 AND b>10485760
| sort - b
```

Understanding this SPL

**Zone Boundary Traffic Monitoring — Anomalous Inter-Zone Volume (IEC 62443-3-2 / Zone boundary traffic monitoring)** — Baselines permitted bytes and sessions between OT zones and flags spikes that may indicate policy drift, covert channels, or misrouted traffic at conduits.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=fw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`, `bytes`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: fw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=fw, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **zone_pair** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, zone_pair, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by zone_pair, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where ratio>8 AND b>10485760` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Zone Boundary Traffic Monitoring — Anomalous Inter-Zone Volume (IEC 62443-3-2 / Zone boundary traffic monitoring)** — Baselines permitted bytes and sessions between OT zones and flags spikes that may indicate policy drift, covert channels, or misrouted traffic at conduits.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=fw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`, `bytes`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (bytes by zone_pair), Heatmap (zone_pair × hour), Table (top spikes).
grandmaExplanation: We baselines permitted bytes and sessions between OT zones and flags spikes that may indicate policy drift, covert channels, or misrouted traffic at conduits. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Network_Traffic data model (Network_Traffic.All_Traffic).
